key_issues: C1 (Critical): get-display-name crashes on `annotations: null` or any non-hash annotations — `(hash-ref (hash-ref md 'annotations (hasheq)) 'title #f)` only defaults on ABSENT annotations; wire null becomes symbol 'null and the inner hash-ref raises. TS's `annotations?.title` tolerates null. Guard rung 2 with hash?; add tests for null/non-hash annotations.

C2 (Critical): json->auth-info decode path has zero adversarial coverage — missing required token/clientId, non-string token, scopes-not-an-array, string/negative expiresAt all untested. Per the project's json->struct self-reject trap, pin the rejection contract and add check-exn tests (missing token/clientId, non-string token).

C3 (Critical): make-auth-info / auth-info/c contracts are unfalsifiable — every test constructs valid values, so the suite stays green even if contracts are missing/wrong. Add construction-side rejection tests: non-string token, negative expires-at, scopes-not-a-list, non-string resource, non-json-object extra.

C4 (Critical): AuthInfo JSON round-trip is vacuous on the decode side for camelCase parity — `(json->auth-info (auth-info->json ai))` passes even if both sides use the wrong key. Add a literal-wire decode test (hand-built hasheq with 'clientId/'expiresAt) to prove the decoder reads camelCase.

C5 (Critical): Part-4 S1-envelope round-trip prerequisite 'minimal valid values' is underspecified — json->request-meta runs clientInfo through json->implementation (needs name+version) and clientCapabilities through json->client-capabilities; a bare-string fixture crashes and masks whether traceparent round-trips. Pin the exact minimal-valid fixture.

S1 (Suggested): M5c's 8-key reserved set differs from S1 request-meta-reserved-keys (which includes progressToken, excludes the 3 trace keys). Add explicit `(check-false (reserved-meta-key? 'progressToken))` to pin this surprising two-notions-of-reserved boundary.

S2 (Suggested): meta-ref/meta-set string-vs-symbol key normalization is tested only for reserved-meta-key?, not the accessors — exercise a string-keyed set readable by the equivalent symbol on a prefixed key (LOG-LEVEL-META-KEY).

S4/S5/S6 (Suggested): untested boundaries — expiresAt=0 (falsy-omit regression) and empty-but-present extra; non-string/null title divergence from 'verbatim TS' (Racket falls through where TS returns the value); pinned 'missing-name raises' never asserted (add check-exn).
